mapping.a_element: extended object being propagated (a classical winding string)
mapping.b_element: extended object being propagated (an NLS soliton)

mapping.a_element: time-dependent background geometry (compactified Milne / contracting-re-expanding orbifold)

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic notion of an extended object moving through a background, but the driving machinery differs: paper A solves the (essentially linear) classical string wave equation via a non-zero-mode expansion to show the trajectory stays non-singular while crossing a cosmological (Milne) singularity, whereas paper B analyzes the nonlinear Schrodinger equation, reducing colliding solitons to effective center-of-mass particle dynamics in a potential with radiation-damping error terms. There is no shared governing equation, no shared threshold/transition, and no coherent role-for-role correspondence between singularity crossing and effective soliton-collision dynamics.
